subject: UX & Research
title: Jargon vs. Plain Language
description: A quick win: understand Jargon vs. Plain Language.

hook.text: A hospital site says 'Schedule an appointment with a provider.' Patients think: 'A doctor? What's a provider?'

buildup.text: Internal jargon leaks into user interfaces constantly. Teams use words they understand that users don't.

discovery.text: The UK's Government Digital Service rewrote all government sites in plain English. Task completion jumped 30%.

twist.text: Even 'settings' vs. 'preferences' vs. 'options' confuses users. Internally different, externally identical.

climax.text: Use the words your users use. Search logs, support tickets, and interviews reveal real user vocabulary.

punchline.text: Speak the user's language, not yours. Plain words win every time.

quiz.question: How can teams discover the right vocabulary for their interface?
options: Analyze search logs, support tickets, and user interviews for real user language
Use industry-standard terminology from professional guides
Copy the language from competitor websites
